name: Dulce Base Full
description: The Dulce Base theory is a conspiracy claiming that a secret, highly advanced biogenetic laboratory and underground facility exists beneath Archuleta Mesa near Dulce, New Mexico. Proponents allege it's a joint human-alien installation, conducting unethical experiments on humans and animals, and housing various extraterrestrial species. The theory gained traction in the late 1980s and early 1990s, fueled by UFO literature and alleged whistleblower testimonies. Core claims involve genetic manipulation, mind control, and collaboration between the U.S. government and alien entities. Supposed evidence includes anecdotal accounts from individuals claiming to have worked at the base (often anonymously or under pseudonyms), alleged blueprints or diagrams of the facility, and purported leaks of classified documents. Mainstream perspectives dismiss the Dulce Base as a hoax or elaborate urban legend. Skeptics point to a lack of verifiable physical evidence, inconsistencies in the narratives, and the absence of corroborating testimonies from credible sources. The geological structure of Archuleta Mesa is known, and extensive drilling or construction of a massive underground complex would likely be detectable through seismic activity or other means. The theory's popularity has been sustained by its connection to broader UFO and alien conspiracy narratives, anxieties about government secrecy, and distrust of scientific advancements. Critics argue the claims often exploit fears surrounding genetic engineering and government overreach. The Dulce Base conspiracy serves as a case study in the spread of misinformation and the power of belief in the face of contradictory evidence.
subtitle: Secret Underground Alien-Human Lab Conspiracy

claims.claim: A secret underground base exists in Dulce, NM.
claims.evidence: Whistleblower testimonies, alleged blueprints, anecdotal accounts.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable physical evidence, inconsistencies in testimonies, geological improbability.

claims.claim: The base is a joint human-alien biogenetic research facility.
claims.evidence: Claims of alien collaboration, accounts of genetic experiments on humans and animals.
claims.criticism: Absence of credible scientific evidence, reliance on unsubstantiated claims, lack of corroboration.

claims.claim: Unethical experiments, including mind control, are conducted at the base.
claims.evidence: Stories of forced implantation, psychological manipulation, and alien control.
claims.criticism: Purely anecdotal accounts, lack of medical or scientific support, easily attributable to psychological factors or misinterpretations.

keyFigures.name: Phil Schneider
keyFigures.role: Alleged whistleblower who claimed to have worked at the base.

keyFigures.name: William Cooper
keyFigures.role: Conspiracy theorist who popularized the Dulce Base narrative.

keyFigures.name: Jerome Clark
keyFigures.role: Ufologist and skeptic who critically examined the Dulce Base claims.

timeline.event: First emergence of the Dulce Base conspiracy theory.
Popularization of the theory through UFO literature and online forums.
Continued dissemination of the theory through alternative media and online communities.

sources.author: Greg Bishop
sources.work: Project Beta: The Story of Paul Bennewitz
sources.title: Greg Bishop
